sources.source_name: Aetna Provider Manual 2025, Chapter 12: Prior Authorization Requirements. Downloaded from aetna.com/providers, March 2025.

REMICADE.notes: Diagnosis must be confirmed by specialist Documentation of failure on conventional therapy required Initial auth: 6 months. Renewal: 12 months. Turnaround: 5 business days standard, 24 hours urgent

KEYTRUDA.notes: PD-L1 testing results required Oncologist letter of medical necessity required Initial auth: 3 months. Renewal: 6 months. Turnaround: 5 business days standard, 24 hours urgent

RITUXAN.notes: CD20 positive status documentation required Initial auth: 6 months. Renewal: 12 months. Turnaround: 5 business days standard, 24 hours urgent

sources.source_name: Phone call to Aetna PA department, (800) 624-0756. Call date: March 22, 2026. Agent: Representative #4412.

data.portal_note: AGENT: Sure, I can help with that. For Remicade prior authorizations, you'll want to submit through our online portal at Availity. That's the preferred method now.

Remicade.notes: AGENT: For Remicade you'll need the PA request form — you can pull that from Availity — chart notes within 90 days, labs, letter of medical necessity, and step therapy documentation showing the patient tried and failed conventional therapy first.

sources.source_name: Scraped from https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/prior-authorization/infusion-medications.html. Scraped date: March 25, 2026.

Remicade.notes: Requires step therapy documentation. Initial auth period: 6 months.

Keytruda.notes: PD-L1 testing required. Initial auth period: 3 months.

Rituxan.notes: CD20+ documentation required. Initial auth period: 6 months.

Entyvio.notes: Requires step therapy documentation. Initial auth period: 6 months.

sources.source_name: Internal database. Denial letter received for case PA-1847, patient PT-55219. Denial date: March 10, 2026.

data.fax_old_status: Submission was received via fax at (888) 267-3277. Please note this fax line is no longer active for infusion medication prior authorizations as of February 2026. Your request was routed manually which caused processing delays.
data.chart_note_policy_update: Additionally, the submitted chart notes are dated November 15, 2025, which exceeds the 90-day documentation window per our updated policy (effective February 1, 2026).

sources.source_name: UnitedHealthcare Provider Manual 2025, Section 8: Prior Authorization for Specialty Drugs. Downloaded from uhcprovider.com, February 2025.

REMICADE.notes: Must document failure of methotrexate or other conventional DMARD (RA indication) Weight-based dosing documentation required Auth period: 6 months initial, 12 months renewal Biosimilar preferred — must document medical rationale if requesting brand

KEYTRUDA.notes: Biomarker testing results (PD-L1, MSI-H, TMB) as applicable Staging documentation Auth period: 3 months, renewable

OCREVUS.notes: MS diagnosis confirmed by neurologist MRI results within 12 months Auth period: 12 months

sources.source_name: Phone call to UnitedHealthcare PA department, (800) 331-6689. Call date: March 18, 2026. Agent: Michael, ID UHC-7831.

data.portal_note: AGENT: Yes, uhcprovider.com. Actually for Remicade specifically, we're asking providers to go through the portal now because we need the biosimilar step therapy documentation in a specific format. The portal has the updated form. If you fax it, make sure you use form UHC-PA-200 — that's the new specialty drug form. The old UHC-PA-100 is being phased out.
data.pa_form_note: AGENT: Yes, uhcprovider.com. Actually for Remicade specifically, we're asking providers to go through the portal now because we need the biosimilar step therapy documentation in a specific format. The portal has the updated form. If you fax it, make sure you use form UHC-PA-200 — that's the new specialty drug form. The old UHC-PA-100 is being phased out.

Remicade.notes: AGENT: Yes, uhcprovider.com. Actually for Remicade specifically, we're asking providers to go through the portal now because we need the biosimilar step therapy documentation in a specific format. The portal has the updated form. If you fax it, make sure you use form UHC-PA-200 — that's the new specialty drug form. The old UHC-PA-100 is being phased out.
CALLER: What docs do we need for Remicade?

AGENT: Chart notes within 60 days, labs, LMN, and you absolutely need the step therapy documentation showing they tried a biosimilar first, or a clear medical justification for why brand Remicade is needed. That's the number one reason we deny Remicade requests.

sources.source_name: Scraped from https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth/infusion-drugs.html. Scraped date: March 20, 2026.

data.chart_note_policy_update: • Chart note window extended to 90 days (previously 60 days)

sources.source_name: Internal database. Denial letter for case PA-2103, patient PT-67432. Denial date: March 5, 2026.

data.chart_note_policy_update: The request for brand Remicade has been denied. Per UnitedHealthcare policy effective January 1, 2026, biosimilar infliximab products must be trialed before brand Remicade will be authorized. No documentation of biosimilar trial or medical justification for brand preference was included in the submission.

sources.source_name: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy Manual, Infusion Therapy Section. Downloaded from cigna.com/providers, November 2024.

REMICADE.notes: Requires documentation of conventional therapy failure Auth period: 6 months (initial), 12 months (renewal) Standard turnaround: 7 business days NOTE: Effective 2025, biosimilar preferred for new starts

ENTYVIO.notes: For Crohn's or UC only — diagnosis must be confirmed Requires failure of anti-TNF therapy OR documented contraindication Auth period: 6 months Standard turnaround: 7 business days

KEYTRUDA.notes: FDA-approved indication documentation required Biomarker testing per NCCN guidelines Auth period: 3 months Standard turnaround: 5 business days

TYSABRI.notes: JCV antibody testing REQUIRED before authorization TOUCH Prescribing Program enrollment verification required Auth period: 12 months Standard turnaround: 7 business days

sources.source_name: Phone call to Cigna PA department, (800) 768-4688. Call date: March 24, 2026. Agent: Denise, ID CG-3390.

data.fax_note: AGENT: For infusion drugs, you want (800) 768-4695. Actually wait — let me check. [pause] OK so there's been a change. For infusion drugs specifically, we have a new dedicated line: (800) 768-4700. The old 4695 number still works but it goes to the general medical PA queue, so it's slower.

sources.source_name: Scraped from https://www.cignaforhcp.com/prior-authorization/infusion-therapy. Scraped date: March 26, 2026.

- Remicade.notes: Biosimilar preferred for new starts effective July 2025

- Tysabri.notes: JCV antibody testing now required every 6 months (previously annual)

- Entyvio.notes: Anti-TNF failure documentation now requires specific drug names and durations

sources.source_name: Internal database. Denial letter for case PA-1955, patient PT-72818. Denial date: March 15, 2026.

data.chart_note_policy_update: 1. Form CG-PA-001 was used. Effective January 2026, infusion drug precertifications require the updated form CG-PA-002. The new form includes required biosimilar attestation fields.

sources.source_name: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield Provider Reference Guide 2025, Medical Drug Authorization section. Downloaded from anthem.com/providers, January 2025.

REMICADE.notes: Step therapy required (biosimilar first for new starts). Prior DMARD trial required for RA indication.

KEYTRUDA.notes: Biomarker documentation. Oncology treatment plan required.

OCREVUS.notes: Neurologist must submit. MRI within 12 months.

HERCEPTIN.notes: HER2 testing results. Biosimilar preferred for new starts.

data.chart_note_policy_update: Additionally, chart notes submitted were dated August 2025, which is outside the 90-day documentation window per our November 2025 policy update.

sources.source_name: Humana Provider Manual, Prior Authorization Guidelines for Injectable & Infusible Drugs, 2024 edition. Downloaded from humana.com/providers, August 2024.

REMICADE.notes: Step therapy required — must trial conventional DMARD first. Biosimilar attestation required for new starts. Auth: 6 months.

RITUXAN.notes: CD20+ documentation. Step therapy required for RA indication only. Auth: 6 months.

ENTYVIO.notes: Anti-TNF failure or contraindication documentation. Auth: 6 months.

OCREVUS.notes: MS specialist must submit. JCV testing recommended. Auth: 12 months.

sources.source_name: Phone call to Humana PA line, (800) 555-2546. Call date: March 19, 2026. Multiple transfer attempts.

data.portal_note: AGENT 3: OK for Rituxan on commercial plans, the fax number is (800) 523-0023. That's the same as it's always been. You can also submit through Availity but honestly our Availity integration has been having issues — I'd fax it if I were you.
data.pa_form_note: AGENT 3: We actually released a new form — HUM-AUTH-2026. It went into effect January 1st. The old form will still get processed but it might get kicked back because it doesn't have the biosimilar attestation section. Let me give you the direct link... actually I don't have it handy. It should be on humana.com/providers under the forms section.

Rituxan.notes: CALLER: Hi Angela, I need to verify the fax number and process for submitting a Rituxan PA for a commercial member.
CALLER: No worries. I need the fax number for Rituxan PA submissions and the current requirements.

AGENT 3: For Rituxan you'll need the PA form, chart notes within 90 days — we changed that from 60, that's on the new form — labs showing CD20 positive, LMN, and if it's for RA you need step therapy documentation. For lymphoma or CLL indications, no step therapy needed.

sources.source_name: Scraped from https://www.humana.com/provider/prior-authorization/medical-drugs. Scraped date: March 21, 2026.
Internal database. Denial for case PA-1888, patient PT-39917. Denial date: March 8, 2026.

data.chart_note_policy_update: 2. OUTDATED DOCUMENTATION: Chart notes provided are dated September 2025 — outside the 90-day window per the January 2026 policy update.
data.denial_reason: REASON: 1. STEP THERAPY NOT MET: For the RA indication, Humana requires documentation of trial and failure of at least one conventional DMARD (e.g., methotrexate) AND one biosimilar rituximab product before brand Rituxan will be authorized. Submission included methotrexate failure documentation but no biosimilar trial or justification. 2. OUTDATED DOCUMENTATION: Chart notes provided are dated September 2025 — outside the 90-day window per the January 2026 policy update. 3. FORM VERSION: Submission used form HUM-AUTH-2024, which does not include the biosimilar attestation section. Please resubmit using HUM-AUTH-2026.
